title: Customers Who Switched Categories
description: Identify customers who made their first purchase in one category and their most recent purchase in a different category. Assume each customer has at most one purchase on a given date.
sql_solution: WITH ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY date) first_rn, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY date DESC) last_rn FROM orders) SELECT customer_id FROM ranked GROUP BY customer_id HAVING MAX(CASE WHEN first_rn = 1 THEN category END) != MAX(CASE WHEN last_rn = 1 THEN category END);
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

first_window = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(to_date(col("date")))
last_window = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(to_date(col("date")).desc())

ranked_orders = (
    orders.withColumn("first_rn", row_number().over(first_window))
    .withColumn("last_rn", row_number().over(last_window))
)

result = (
    ranked_orders.groupBy("customer_id")
    .agg(
        max(when(col("first_rn") == 1, col("category"))).alias("first_category"),
        max(when(col("last_rn") == 1, col("category"))).alias("last_category")
    )
    .filter(col("first_category") != col("last_category"))
    .select("customer_id")
)
